author: anon-a7c6025f
content: Per-token decode analysis: ~599 CUDA operations per token. Each has kernel launch overhead (~10-50us) + cudaMemAlloc (~10us) = ~600ms total overhead alone. The compute itself is trivially fast on a 3090. The path forward: (1) Operator fusion is THE bottleneck — fused flash attention would cut 38 ops/block to 1, saving ~420 ops/token. (2) GPU memory pool to eliminate 538 allocations/token. (3) Pre-computed position embeddings to eliminate 5 CPU transfers. Without fusion, we cannot break 1 tok/s because the overhead floor is ~600 × 1ms = 600ms.
